authorization.scope: exact persistence restoration for an already-validated unit quaternion

discovered_gap.fact: UnitQuaternion::new_xyzw renormalizes its input, and renormalizing ordinary already-valid quaternion components can change their IEEE-754 bits.
discovered_gap.risk: A project save/open/save cycle could drift canonical camera and cross-section bytes even when the user made no edit.
rejected_workarounds: bypass the private representation with unsafe code
move quaternion reconstruction outside the project-store authority

correction.validation: require finite components, positive-zero encoding, canonical quaternion sign, and squared norm within sixteen f64 epsilon of one; preserve accepted bits exactly
correction.error: add one typed GeometryError variant for a non-canonical stored quaternion
correction.supersedes: the dependency correction's domain-unchanged invariant only for this one method and error variant

evidence: a concrete ordinary quaternion whose second normalization changes bits
fixed-seed property coverage proving every constructor output restores bit-exactly
rejection of non-finite, non-unit, negative-sign, and negative-zero encodings
the independent WP-10B project fixture round-trips nontrivial camera and cross-section orientations
stop_condition: Any other domain type, dependency, invariant, or public root must change.
rollback: revert this correction and the method before accepting the B1 wire contract; no runtime or persistence authority is active yet
